headline: Week of chaos: OpenAI kills GPT-5.1, Claude dominates the App Store amid #QuitGPT backlash, and DeepSeek V4 arrives with 1T parameters

stories.title: OpenAI retires GPT-5.1, launches GPT-5.3 Instant + GPT-5.4 Thinking/Pro
stories.summary: <cite index="2-2,2-3">OpenAI released three new models in the GPT-5 family—GPT-5.3 Instant for fast everyday work, GPT-5.4 Thinking for difficult professional tasks, and GPT-5.4 Pro for the most demanding work</cite>. <cite index="1-1,1-2">GPT-5.1 models were retired and auto-migrated to GPT-5.3 or GPT-5.4 variants as of March 11, 2026</cite>.
stories.takeaway: OpenAI's pushing out new family variants but lowkey feels reactive—like they're shuffling deck chairs after the Pentagon drama

stories.title: Claude becomes #1 on App Store amid #QuitGPT boycott; Anthropic launches Claude Partner Network
stories.summary: <cite index="4-4,4-5">2.5 million users abandoned ChatGPT and Claude became #1 on the App Store following OpenAI's Pentagon deal</cite>. <cite index="11-4,11-6">Anthropic launched the Claude Partner Network with a $100 million 2026 commitment to support partners with training and technical support</cite>.
stories.takeaway: Anthropic's capitalizing on the chaos—building ecosystem support while OpenAI deals with the boycott fallout fr
stories.source: Anthropic News + MML Studio AI Weekly

stories.title: DeepSeek V4 launches with 1T parameters, 40% memory reduction via MODEL1 architecture
stories.summary: <cite index="5-1,5-2,5-3">DeepSeek V4 launched around March 3 with 1 trillion parameters and MODEL1 architecture that cuts memory by 40%, plus Sparse FP8 decoding achieves 1.8x inference speedup</cite>. Context window expanded to 1M+ tokens.
stories.takeaway: China's making serious moves—DeepSeek's efficiency gains hit different, lowkey threatening the compute-cost advantage US labs relied on

stories.title: Google DeepMind partners with DOE on Genesis Mission; launches AlphaEvolve for advanced algorithm design
stories.summary: <cite index="23-19,23-20,23-21">Google DeepMind launched an accelerated access program for scientists at 17 DOE National Labs with AI co-scientist on Google Cloud—a multi-agent virtual scientific collaborator built on Gemini designed to help scientists synthesize vast amounts of information to generate novel hypotheses</cite>. <cite index="23-5">AlphaEvolve, a Gemini-powered coding agent for designing advanced algorithms, is being expanded for National Laboratories</cite>.
stories.takeaway: Google's betting hard on science—partnering with government labs to prove AI can actually accelerate research beyond the hype
stories.source: Google DeepMind Blog + Brookhaven National Lab

watchNext.title: Claude 5 (Fennec) leak: coding abilities surpassing Opus 4.6, 50% cheaper pricing expected
watchNext.why: Leaks suggest Claude 5 could drop Feb/March 2026 with major coding + reasoning upgrades; literally the model everyone's waiting for after the Anthropic ban

watchNext.title: Meta's Mango (video generation) + Avocado (LLM) targeting H1 2026 release from Meta Superintelligence Labs
watchNext.why: Meta's first flagship models post-reorganization—Mango aims for world models + video, Avocado for coding; could shake up the market if they actually ship
